label: Structured-decoding runtimes

node.label: Structured-decoding runtimes
node.definition: Structured-decoding runtimes compile grammars or schemas into efficient token filters integrated with a model server’s decoding loop.
node.why_it_matters: They make reliable structured generation practical at serving scale instead of relying only on slow application-side retries.
limitations: Grammar compilation, tokenizer interaction, and per-request constraints can reduce throughput or complicate batching.

dependencies.rationale: Structured-decoding runtimes turns Grammar- and schema-constrained decoding into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Structured-decoding runtimes turns Model servers & inference runtimes into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
Structured-decoding runtimes turns Graph compilers, runtimes & operator fusion into a scalable, deployable, observable, or integrity-preserving capability.
